3A_Bibliographic.citation: Brown, S. A., Garavan, H., Jernigan, T. L., Tapert, S. F., Huber, R. S., Lopez, D., Murray, T., Dowling, G., Hoffman, E. A., & Uddin, L. Q. (2025). Responsible use of population neuroscience data in the ABCD: Towards standards of accountability and integrity. European Journal of Neuroscience, 61, e16662.
3A_Bibliographic.notes: THIS IS AN EDITORIAL, NOT AN EMPIRICAL STUDY. It does not contain original data analysis, effect sizes, or primary research findings. It discusses responsible use of ABCD data. Should be EXCLUDED from the systematic review.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: N/A - This is an editorial about responsible data use, not an empirical study.

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: We therefore urge researchers to attend to the recommendations provided here and elsewhere to help ensure fair, inclusive and just science.
3H_Recommendations.notes: Recommendations are about responsible data use, not about screen time or social media.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: This is an editorial about responsible use of ABCD data. It discusses the importance of avoiding stigmatization of marginalized groups, contextualizing social constructs like race, and ensuring ethical data practices. It does not present any original empirical findings about screen time or social media.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: This paper should be excluded from the systematic review as it is an editorial/commentary, not an empirical study examining screen time/social media outcomes.
